snippet.description:

This video would delve into the various aspects of David L. Calhoun's leadership style and how it may have contributed to the Boeing crisis. It would analyze his decision-making process, communication skills, and overall management approach, providing a comprehensive look at his leadership during this challenging time.

"The Boeing Crisis: Assessing David L. Calhoun's Crisis Management Strategies"

This video would focus on the crisis management strategies employed by David L. Calhoun in response to the Boeing crisis. It would explore his ability to handle the situation, including his crisis communication skills, decision-making under pressure, and efforts to restore public trust in the company.

"The Fallout of the Boeing Crisis: Examining David L. Calhoun's Accountability"

This video would examine the level of accountability demonstrated by David L. Calhoun in the aftermath of the Boeing crisis. It would analyze his response to the crisis, including any apologies, changes implemented, and efforts to rectify the situation. The video would also discuss the impact of his accountability on the company's reputation and future prospects.

"Leadership Lessons from the Boeing Crisis: What Can We Learn from David L. Calhoun?"

This video would explore the leadership lessons that can be derived from David L. Calhoun's handling of the Boeing crisis. It would highlight both the successes and failures of his leadership approach, providing valuable insights for viewers interested in leadership and crisis management.

"The Future of Boeing: Can David L. Calhoun Lead the Company to Recovery?"

This video would focus on the future prospects of Boeing under David L. Calhoun's leadership. It would assess his ability to navigate the company through the crisis and lead it towards recovery. The video would discuss the challenges ahead, potential strategies, and the expectations placed on Calhoun as the CEO.

snippet.title: He Was Enslaved by Jefferson Davis. Here’s What He Said
snippet.description:
My favorite books: https://www.amazon.com/shop/nytn?ref_=cm_sw_r_cp_ud_aipsfshop_80SZ55GVN3PE27XRN3YB_1 #civilwar #findingyourroots #ancestrydna  #africanamerican  #familystory #genealogy #hiddenstories #history 

 Dive into a riveting 1931 interview with George Johnson, a man who had the unbelievable experience of being owned by Confederate President Jefferson Davis. Amidst the crackling audio, Johnson challenges our understanding of American history, offering a perspective that's as uncomfortable as it is important.

____
Want to support this project? https://www.patreon.com/NYTN/about

Want to rewatch any of "Finding Lola"? Here's the series: 

Watch the Episode 1 that started the whole journey:
https://youtu.be/SQp7jeNp_yg

Watch Episode 2 here: 
https://youtu.be/qPzPKSJfkeo

Watch Episode 3 here: 
https://youtu.be/bLxaTBhCu_Y

Watch Episode 4 here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WDz6Fgr3qlY


--------
Come join me on a new docu-series that explores identity, racial tensions in the South during the 20th century, and the unique experiences of those who historically called Louisiana home.
My name is Danielle Romero, and all my life, I have romanticized Louisiana.
Growing up in New York, it represented a place where I could step back the sepia-toned life of my great grandmother, Lola Perot, who died before I was born.
Now, it was time to go back to Louisiana--although I had no idea what the truth would be or what questions to ask---who was Lola really? Who were we?

snippet.description:
From Season two!
Entire episode playlists available on my youtube channel page!

Science fiction/Action adventure/Space. Created by Gene Roddenberry. Starring William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, DeForest Kelley (Kirk, Spock and Dr. McCoy) - Norway Productions and Desilu Productions produced the series from September 1966 to December 1967. Paramount Television produced the show from January 1968 to June 1969. Star Trek aired on NBC from September 8, 1966, to June 3, 1969. It was first broadcast on September 6, 1966, on Canada's CTV network. Star Trek's Nielsen ratings while on NBC were low, and the network cancelled it after three seasons and 79 episodes. Several years later, the series became a hit in broadcast syndication, remaining so throughout the 1970s, achieving cult classic status and a developing influence on popular culture. Star Trek eventually spawned a franchise, consisting of eight television series, 13 feature films, and numerous books, games, and toys, and is now widely considered one of the most popular and influential television series of all time.

snippet.title: Kowloon Walled City: The Most Terrifying Place on Earth?
snippet.description: Kowloon Walled City was a densely populated and unregulated urban enclave located in Hong Kong. Originally a military fortress, it became a haven for refugees and criminals in the 20th century. By the 1980s, it had become infamous for its lawlessness and squalid living conditions, with over 50,000 residents crammed into its 6.5-acre area. The city was finally demolished in 1993, and the site was transformed into a park. Despite its controversial past, Kowloon Walled City remains a fascinating case study in urban planning and social history.

snippet.title: Tour Jennifer Dubois' Afro Caribbean Sanctuary in San Diego | How We Live Ep 1
snippet.description:
In this house tour we meet with Jennifer DuBois, a professional organizer and nursing professor living in San Diego, CA. Jennifer shares her unique style, which blends neutral decor with Afro-Caribbean influences.

As we explore Jennifer's space, we see how she has created a sense of connection to Africa through her carefully curated decor. From wood carvings to masks and bold pops of color, Jennifer's home feels collected and authentic.
